column1: # Vat.gr.1291, f.6r — Ptolemy, Handy Tables: Oblique Ascensions for Klima V (Byzantion, longest day = 15h)
# Header: ΚΛΙΜΑΤΟΣ ΔΙΑ ΒΥΖΑΝΤΙΟΥ ΠΑΡΑΛΛΗΛΟΥ ὧ ἴση ἡ μεγίστη ἡμέρα ιε ...
# This folio contains across the opening: ΠΑΡΘΕΝΟΥ (Virgo, end), ΖΥΓΟΥ (Libra), ΣΚΟΡΠΙΟΥ (Scorpio), ΤΟΞΟΤΟΥ (Sagittarius)
# Note: Virgo column is partially visible on the right edge of image 3 (5v right side)
# Values are CUMULATIVE from start of Aries (vernal equinox)
# === ΠΑΡΘΕΝΟΥ (Virgo) — visible on right edge of image 3 (5v right) ===
# Note: I can only see the Anaphorai column clearly; other columns partially obscured
# Note: At end of Virgo (autumnal equinox point on ecliptic) cumulative ascension should reach 180° but here we are at ~130;36° —
# This is because the table shows OBLIQUE ascensions (anaphorai), not right ascensions.
# Symmetry check: end-of-Virgo value should equal 360° - (start-of-Aries reset).
# Actually 130;36 here means a different counting system or table reset point.
# Note: values appear to RESET (the table seems to restart counting from Libra=autumnal equinox onward)
# This is a feature of how some Byzantine versions structured the tables.
